data.title: Taiwan Semi not subject of US probe over AI chip curbs on Huawei
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) said it alerted the U.S. government about a potential attempt to have it manufacture AI chips for Huawei Technologies.

"We proactively communicated with the US Commerce Department regarding the matter in the report. We are not aware of TSMC being the subject of any investigation at this time," said Taiwan Semiconductor said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha. "In compliance with the regulatory requirements, TSMC has not supplied to Huawei since mid-September 2020."

Last week, TSM said it was committed to complying with all applicable rules and regulations, including applicable export control following a report that the U.S. Commerce Department was probing if the Taiwanese foundry was making AI or smartphone chips for the Chinese company Huawei, and violating U.S. export rules for China.

TSM reiterated in its email on Tuesday that it is a law-abiding company and was committed to complying with all applicable rules and regulations, including applicable export controls.

The company noted that it maintains a comprehensive export system for monitoring and ensuring compliance. "If we have any reason to believe there are potential issues, we will take prompt action to ensure compliance, including conducting investigations and proactively communicating with relevant parties including customers and regulatory authorities as necessary," TSM stated.

TSM recently notified the Department after a customer placed orders for a chip that resembled Huawei's chip, Ascend 910B, The Financial Times reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Huawei's Ascend 910B chips are used in China to train large language models, or LLMs, that power AI-chatbots, and were made to rival chips from Nvidia (NVDA). Nvidia and other American companies do not supply advanced chips to China due to U.S. export curbs. According to a prior report, a Huawei executive had said that around half of China's LLMs were trained with Huawei's chips and that 910B's performance had surpassed Nvidia's A100 in training models.

Taiwan Semi made the precursor of the 910B chip before the U.S. export restrictions sanctions came into effect, according to the FT report.

After getting an order that raised doubts, TSM talked to the customer involved and also with the Commerce Department, the report noted, citing a person close to TSM. The department's probe on the issue would be "related to" TSM but the company would not be the target of an investigation, the report added.

There had been "conversations" between TSM and the Department about a potential attempt at bypassing the export curbs but there was no indication of malicious compliance violations on TSM's part, the report added citing a person with knowledge of the matter.

data.title: Washington Trust Bancorp raised to Neutral at Piper after Q3 earnings beat; stock climbs
data.description:
Washington Trust Bancorp (NASDAQ:WASH) stock gapped up 5.7% in Tuesday late morning trading after posting better-than-expected Q3 earnings and revenue, which spurred Piper Sandler analyst Mark Fitzgibbon to upgrade the regional bank to Neutral from Underweight.

The Q3 report was highlighted by a modest sequential increase in net interest margin and net interest income, although Washington (WASH) recorded a slight decline in noninterest income and total lending.

Fitzgibbon raised his Q4 EPS estimate to $0.62 from $0.58 (vs. $0.54 average analyst estimate), reflecting "our expectations for somewhatlower credit costs and higher fee revenues than previously anticipated."

The stock's relative underperformance this year also stoked the analyst's decision for a rating upgrade.

data.title: Altair Engineering jumps on report it's exploring sale after takeover interest
data.description:
Altair Engineering (NASDAQ:ALTR) quickly soared 10% after a report that the engineering software maker is exploring a sale after receiving takeover interest.

Altair (ALTR), which has a market cap of $8 billion, is working with investment bankers to gauge interest from possible bidders, according to a Reuters report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. If talks are successful, a deal could be reached in the coming weeks.

Potential bidders include rival design software makers such as Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), according to the report. Reuters couldn't determine whether Cadence had approached Altair.

Developing story ...

data.title: Wolfspeed could see upside from separating its devices unit, activist Jana says
data.description:
Chipmaker Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) could see upside to its share price if it would separate its devices unit, Scott Ostfeld, managing partner at activist investment firm Jana Partners, said. Wolfspeed shares rose 1.8%.

Jana has a "significant stake in Wolfspeed," Ostfelfd said at the 13D Monitor Active-Passive Investor Summit in New York City on Tuesday, according to a Bloomberg account of the event.

Wolfspeed could also look to partially monetize its silicon carbide unit, Ostfeld added.

Ostfeld's comments come after Wolfspeed (WOLF) last Wednesday said it nominated two directors to its board for election at the annual meeting in December. The silicon carbide maker nominated Thomas Seifert and Woody Young for board election at the company's annual meeting on Dec. 5.

The board nominations came as activist Jana has a sizeable stake in Wolfspeed (WOLF) and has reportedly pushed for the company to explore it to explore its options, including a potential sale. The activist push comes after Jana first disclosed a stake in Wolfspeed (WOLF) in November of last year.

“We applaud the additions of Woody and Tom to the board and believe they will enhance the board’s efforts to improve operational execution and unlock Wolfspeed’s strategic value,” Ostfeld said in a statement emailed to Seeking Alpha at the time.

data.title: Higher prices bolster profits at Kimberly-Clark as sales soften
data.description:
Driven by higher prices for its personal care products, Kimberly-Clark’s (NYSE:KMB) profits improved by 5% from a year ago and beat Wall Street’s expectations by $0.12 per share. But while profits improved, the higher prices drove consumers to cheaper alternatives, sending overall sales down 4% year-over-year.

“Price-led gains reflected necessary pricing actions to address higher local costs in hyperinflationary economies, mainly in Argentina,” the company said, addressing the need to raise prices.

In North America, organic sales were down 1%, attributed to a 3% decline in K-C Professional and 1% decline in the company’s consumer tissue segment. By segment, personal care sales were down 2% in the quarter and profits in the category were down 4% due to higher advertising costs.

The company’s adjusted profit margin improved 90 basis points to 36.7%.

In the consumer tissue segment, sales were down 2% while operating profit of $265M decreased by 1%. Sales in the company’s K-C Professional segment were down 10%, while profits fell 4% to $161M.

Looking ahead to the remainder of the year, Kimberly-Clark now expects organic sales to increase by 3%-4% versus a mid-single digit growth rate, previously, reflecting headwinds from changes in retail inventory levels. Adjusted earnings per share are still expected to grow at a mid-to-high teens percentage rate.

Shares are down more than 4% to a 12-week low, weighing on other names in the category with Unilever (UL) down 0.7%, Church & Dwight (CHD) down 1.5%, and Kenvue (KVUE) lower by 0.5%.

data.title: Interpublic stock sinks after lackluster Q3 report
China’s consumer confidence continues to gradually slip – MS
data.description:
Morgan Stanley’s AlphaWise Consumer Pulse Survey in late September showed that sentiment for China’s economy remains weak.

The survey encompassed 2,000 consumer respondents and found that confidence in China’s economy slipped, said Katy L. Huberty, the global head of research at Morgan Stanley, in a note.

The survey showed that 49% of consumers expect the Chinese economy to improve in the next six months. But the percentage is down 6 percentage points from 55% in June.

About 13% of respondents anticipate the Chinese economy to get worse, up 1 percentage point from June; and about 38% of respondents said they expect the economy to stay the same, up 5 percentage points from June.

In addition, household spending intentions continued to trend down, the survey showed, with 25% of consumers expecting to spend more in the next quarter, down 3 percentage points quarter-over-quarter. About 10% expect to spend less – 2 percentage points higher quarter-over-quarter.

Early this week, China cut the benchmark lending rates by 25 basis points to 3.10% as a part of a package of stimulus measures to boost its economy. The five-year loan prime rate was also cut 25 basis points to 3.6%, and the one-year loan prime rate was cut to 3.10%.

“Our China economists believe the weakening consumer sentiment points to a need for further consumption stimulus to help alleviate the deflation spiral,” said Huberty.

China ETFs: (NASDAQ:MCHI), (FXI), (GXC).

data.title: Peloton falls as Costco partnership is viewed cautiously
data.description:
Peloton Interactive (NASDAQ:PTON) jumped fell back from an early gain after the company announced a new partnership with Costco (NASDAQ:COST) to sell the Peloton Bike+ online and in 300 of its U.S. stores this holiday season.

Starting November 1, Costco (COST) members will be able to purchase an exclusive Bike+ bundle through February 15, 2025. "The Costco brand is incredibly powerful and respected in the US and abroad. Costco members know they are shopping for high-quality products from trusted brands with the best value," stated Dion Camp Sanders, Chief Emerging Business Officer, Peloton.

The Peloton Bike+, which is self-assembly, is expected to retail for $1,999 in-store and $2,199 online (including delivery), and comes with a 48-month extended warranty. The All-Access Membership, required to access content on the Peloton Bike+ for $44 USD/month, allows members to create accounts for the entire household. The Membership provides unlimited access to Peloton’s content library with 16 modalities from cycling to bike bootcamp, strength, pilates, meditation, and more.

Analysts on Wall Street expressed some concern about the implications of Peloton (PTON) striking another retail deal, which could end up cutting margins.

Shares of Peloton Interactive (PTON) were down 2.4% in Tuesday morning trading as investors viewed the Costco (COST) partnership announcement with some skepticism.
